title: pi-stories — Design Decisions
description: Before I build, let me validate the key decisions. I've studied Overstory's 32-command, 4-database architecture and I know where they're strong and where you can beat them.

questions.question: Core philosophy — what makes pi-stories fundamentally different from Overstory?

options.label: Blueprint Engine native — [D]/[N] interleaving is the core, not bolted on

code.code:
# Overstory: spawn agent, hope for the best
ov sling task-1 --capability builder

# pi-stories: deterministic gates BUILT INTO the pipeline
pi-stories run task-1
# [D] context.json -> [N] scout -> [D] lint -> [N] build -> [D] test -> [N] review -> [D] commit

options.label: Cost-first — every command shows token cost, auto-routes to cheapest model

code.code:
# Scout on Haiku ($0.001), build on Sonnet ($0.01), review on Opus ($0.10)
pi-stories run task-1 --budget $2.00
# Auto-selects models per phase based on budget

options.label: Both — Blueprint Engine + cost routing as twin pillars

code.code:
pi-stories run task-1 --budget $2.00
# [D] pre-compute -> [N] scout (haiku) -> [D] lint
# -> [N] build (sonnet) -> [D] test -> [N] review (opus)
# -> [D] commit -> cost report

questions.recommended: Both — Blueprint Engine + cost routing as twin pillars

questions.question: Scope — how big is v0.1?

options.label: Minimal — 5 commands: init, run, status, costs, doctor

code.code:
pi-stories init          # creates .pi-stories/ in project
pi-stories run <task>    # Blueprint Engine pipeline
pi-stories status        # what's running
pi-stories costs         # token/cost breakdown
pi-stories doctor        # health check

options.label: Medium — 10 commands: add mail, trace, replay, merge, dashboard

code.code:
# Core 5 + observability
pi-stories trace <run-id>
pi-stories replay <run-id>
pi-stories mail send/check
pi-stories merge
pi-stories dashboard

options.label: Full — match Overstory's 32 commands from day 1

questions.recommended: Minimal — 5 commands: init, run, status, costs, doctor

questions.question: Runtime — what do we build with?

options.label: TypeScript + Bun (like Overstory — fast, bun:sqlite built-in)
Plain bash + jq (Mario's philosophy — zero dependencies)

code.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# pi-stories run
context=$(git diff --name-only main..HEAD | jq -R -s 'split("\n")')
pi "Read context.json and implement" --model haiku

questions.recommended: TypeScript + Bun (like Overstory — fast, bun:sqlite built-in)

questions.question: Agent dispatch — how do we spawn agents?

options.label: pi subagents (built-in, uses existing agent definitions)
interactive_shell dispatch (supports ANY CLI — pi, claude, codex, gemini)
Direct CLI spawn via Bun.spawn (lowest level, most control)

questions.recommended: Direct CLI spawn via Bun.spawn (lowest level, most control)

questions.question: Where does it live?

options.label: GitHub repo: arosstale/pi-stories (public, clone to use)
Pi skill in pi-doodlestein (private, your setup only)

questions.recommended: GitHub repo: arosstale/pi-stories (public, clone to use)

questions.question: Name confirmation

options.label: pi-stories — stories like user stories, each run tells a story
pi-forge — forging code through deterministic + non-deterministic steps
pi-minions — from the Stripe Minions paper that inspired the architecture
pi-conductor — orchestrating agents like a conductor

questions.recommended: pi-stories — stories like user stories, each run tells a story
